series_metadata.series_title: Real Estate, Appraiser, Home Inspector, AMC and Cemetery Files - No Further Action - Public Records
series_metadata.series_description: This retention schedule pertains to records received by the Division regarding a complaint against a registration, license or certification relating to appraisers, appraisal management companies, a person engaged in unlicensed activity (ORC 4768.14, 4764.16 or 4735.052), real estate licensees, foreign real estate licensees, home inspector licensees or cemeteries which resulted in the Division taking no further action or withdrawing charges resulting in no further action. This retention schedule consists of, but is not limited to, the complaint, letters notifying the parties no further action will be taken, any proof of service, any appeal responses, any exhibits submitted to the Board or Commission for its review, any Board or Commission orders or any related correspondence.

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Paper records are retained on-site until the disciplinary file is closed at the administrative level - the date of the Division''''''''s final notice or the date of the Board or Commission Order, whichever is later. Then the paper records may be maintained off-site for five years after the disciplinary file is closed at the administrative level - the date of the Division''''''''s final notice or the date of the Board or Commission Order, whichever is later. Then paper record may be recycled. ; Electronic: For any records maintained in machine readable format, the records shall be maintained five years after the disciplinary file is closed on the administrative level - date of the Division''''''''s final notice or the date of the Board or Commission Order, whichever is later. Then the record shall be destroyed. ; Microform: For any records maintained in microform, the records shall be maintained five years after the disciplinary file is closed on the administrative level - date of the Division''''''''s final notice or the date of the Board or Commission Order, whichever is later. Then the microform shall be destroyed., Paper: Recycle ; Electronic: Shred ; Microform: Recycle
